meaning: n.在灰泥的墙壁上作的水彩画,壁画vt.作壁画于
examples: It was quite a posh al fresco affair: the waiter was even wearing a shirt, unlike many of his compatriots.
A fresco painting of the 17th century.
He turned to Teddy and asked, “What do you think of this old fresco?”
An open-air boardwalk called The Waterfront offers al fresco dining and shopping venues with seaside views.
We are experiencing the dissolution of the pleasing fresco represented by the old market segmentation model.
